entry.title: CX Onboarding: How to Prevent and Manage Project Risks Using SAP Best Practices
entry.content:
How to Prevent and Manage Project Risks Using SAP Best Practices

Implementing SAP Customer Experience (CX) solutions offers organizations a powerful opportunity to modernize processes, streamline operations, and strengthen customer engagement.

Read below to learn how customers and partners can avoid the most common project pitfalls, which we have grouped into three major risk areas: project‑related, people‑related, and security/compliance‑related risks.

Setting a Strong Foundation for Project Success and Managing Project‑Related Risks

Effective risk management starts long before configuration begins. One of the first steps is choosing the right implementation methodology to guide your project from initiation through day‑to‑day operations. The recommended approach is SAP Activate, which includes extensive training resources to help you become familiar with the framework. SAP also offers the SAP Activate Road Map Viewer (S‑User required), where you can access templates and assets that support every phase from Discover to Run.

Once you’ve selected the right methodology, your project is already on a strong footing. However, there are additional considerations to help you proactively prevent risks:

  • Perform upfront risk assessments and budget planning to avoid budget overruns.
  • Define a clear scope, timeline, and responsibility matrix
  • Document and validate end‑to‑end business processes
  • Leverage SAP’s solutions such as:
    • SAP Signavio for process visualization
    • SAP LeanIX for understanding IT landscapes and dependencies

Adopting a Clean Core Strategy for Long‑Term Stability

With the rise of cloud technologies, companies can innovate at a faster pace than ever before. Within this context, maintaining a clean core becomes essential. By minimizing custom code in the core system, organizations can ensure stability, simplify upgrades, and improve extensibility.

A clean core keeps your cloud environment streamlined and efficient, avoiding the unnecessary complexity that often results from excessive customizations.

👉How to maintain a clean core:

  • Use SAP for Me and SAP Cloud ALM to track system health and core integrity
  • Prefer configuration over customization whenever possible
  • Use SAP Business Technology Platform for modular extensions
  • Implement strong governance for evaluating and approving changes

A clean core plays a key role in reducing technical risks and building operational resilience, innovation readiness, and total cost efficiency.

Project risks may arise not only from technical gaps, such as lack of know‑how, product issues, or configuration challenges, but also from misalignment, late decisions, or communication gaps. These are best addressed by setting up proper Change Management alongside traditional Project Management.

Managing People‑Related Risks: Addressing the Human Side of Change with Organizational Change Management

Project management and change management play different but complementary roles. Cloud transformation affects people just as much as processes, and without a clear change management methodology, even small adjustments can meet resistance and slow progress.

Establishing a well‑defined change management strategy helps address these concerns and supports the human side of the project.  SAP supports this with the Organizational Change Management Road Map Viewer (S-User required), aligned with SAP Activate and filled with templates and accelerators. Effective change management improves communication, supports training, and reduces unexpected scope changes.

👉How to prevent resistance to new cloud processes:

  • Run early change and readiness assessments to understand your organization’s capacity for change
  • Create a compelling change story that explains the shift from current state to future benefits
  • Set up a communication strategy for continuous, transparent collaboration between stakeholders (e.g., business and IT), supporting feedback loops and efficient updates

 Security and Data Compliance Risks: A Part of Your Project That Cannot Be Overlooked

Security, privacy, and compliance are critical topics that must be addressed early. Delays in these areas can significantly impact project timelines and outcomes.

SAP provides the Trust Center, your central platform for security and compliance, delivering full transparency and easy access to SAP trust-related documents. In the Trust Center, you can access information on cloud service status, security, privacy, compliance, cloud operations, data centers, and agreements.

👉Some fundamentals when it comes to security:

  • Identify internal security, compliance, and data protection contacts early
  • Share the SAP Trust Center with them to provide access to key documents and certificates
  • Join SAP’s Security Community to stay informed about best practices
  • Subscribe to the SAP Security Products and Solutions Newsletter to keep up with product updates, events, and relevant news

 User Adoption and What Happens After Go‑Live

A technically sound system cannot deliver value if end users are unsure, unprepared, or unconvinced. When IT and business stakeholders don’t collaborate closely, organizations may encounter inconsistent usage, incomplete data entry, or a continued reliance on old tools.

By establishing a strong Change Management foundation, you prepare the way for successful user adoption after go‑live. That includes:

  • Nominating Product Ambassadors who champion adoption and gather ongoing feedback
  • Addressing user concerns proactively and incorporating feedback into project decisions
  • Defining metrics and KPIs to measure successful adoption (for example, the Consumption tab under Finance & Legal in SAP for Me provides insights on productive system usage)
  • Considering WalkMe as a digital adoption platform, offering interactive guidance to support onboarding and daily use

By taking these points into account, you set your project on the right path to success, helping you navigate and minimize risks throughout your cloud transformation.

 

Want to learn more?

📢Hear from our experts in the CX Onboarding: Managing Project Risks webcast — check our schedule.
📞You can also schedule a 1:1 call with one of our team members.

Key Resources

  • SAP Activate Road Map Viewer
  • Discovering SAP Activate - Implementation Tools and Methodology
  • Adoption Best Practices Video and Presentation
  • Organizational Change Management Road Map Viewer

We’d love to hear from you! Visit our Onboarding Resource Center, or feel free to contact us: SAP_CX_Onboarding@sap.com.

entry.title: Explain Central finance important things.
entry.content:
➢ Introductions: - With Central finance we can transition to SAP S/4 HANA without disruption to our current system landscape, which can be made up of a combination of sap system of different releases. This allows you to establish a central reporting platform for FICO with the option of creating a common reporting structure.

➢ Central Finance structure: -

 

Lest say we have SAP system of any released and non-SAP system, The Data is extracted to these servers to SLT server called “SAP landscape Transformation replication server”, It than goes to the different processes like first accounting interface of central finance where a lot of business mapping rules are defined, and we need to spend an ample of time during implementation. If records do not fall under the mapping rule, then it should be landed at error handling call AIR“Application Interface Framework” & if records are good to go as per business mapping rule, then document posted in Central finance ACDOCA – Universal Journal through internal accounting interface.

Why we plan for central finance?
Ans. Companies has multiple ERP systems, Companies doing multiple merger and Acquisitions, Companies have multiple non-SAP systems, non-disruptive structure and leading Finance First as single reporting structure.

➢ Key Important Things: - During implementation of central finance, financial transactions from the source are replicated to Central finance and most important and critical area is Master data.

▪ Financial & Non-Financial Master data
          • GL Account – Financial
          • Cost Element – Financial
          • Cost Centers – Financial
          • Profit Centers – Financial
          • Internal Order and Production Order etc. – Financial
          • Customer and Vendor (BP) – Non-Financial
          • Plants - non-financial
          • Materials – non-financial

▪ Initial Load: - Based Financial and Non-financial data we will plan initial load to CFIN system and some key important things we need to take care.

▪ Field Status Group: T code OBC4: - Filed status group determines which filed are suppressed, required or optional during document entry. If source has different field than target, then consider setting target as optional. Important you can map both system is same but more issue if mismatched Require and suppress Vs Other status between source and target system.

▪ Open item management: - Always consider Open item mgt. to Open Item Mgt. and Non-Open item Mgt. to non-open item Mgt… Check SAP notes based on system if ECC check 175960 – Activation of open item management and for S4 2745769 – Activation of open item
management. Program- FINS_SWITCH_TO_OPEN_ITEM: -

 

 

▪ Primary cost element: - Primary cost and Revenue includes P&L account like expenses, depreciation, salaries, revenue.

 

 

▪ Secondary cost element: - Secondary cost element are now GL.

 

 

▪ Non-Operating account: - P&L without cost element is now non-operating account and it may be use for Treasury, Foreign exchange gain & loss and Work in progress business scenarios.

 

 

▪ Reconciliation Account: - Reconciliation accounts are GL account to which postings are made automatically whenever a business transaction is entered on a subledger account (Such as accounts receivable, Account payable, and Fixed asset) Asset accounting is not supported in CFIN, the asset reconciliation accounts are mapped to non-reconciliation accounts. Replicated FI documents from asset postings are posted to general ledger in CFIN.
Entries available in ACDOCA table. Customer / Vendor reconciliation accounts are mapped to reconciliation accounts in CFIN.

 

▪ Tax category: - Source and target tax category should be same as Input, output and empty including posting without tax allow field check box ticked and not tucked.


▪ GR/IR Clearing account: - If business require enhancement, then check SAP note 165692 – Documentation Enhancement LMR1M002.

Note: - Once mappings are done and initial load also done then SAP provide important transaction code to check the loaded data status in CFIN.

 

▪ Margin Analysis: - Margine Analysis is huge topic but I will cover key important things. ACODA single source of truth.

 

 

 

 

▪ Initial load: - First reduce number of open items in source system before starting initial load and use transaction code like F-32, F-44 , F-03, F.13, FB1SL etc.

Initial load setting defines in table VCFIN_SOURCE_SET.

 

➢ AIF “Application Interface framework”: - AIF is powerful tool that helps execute error handling. Standard reports are below.

 

 

➢ Key takeaway & Important sap notes: - Data mapping and cleansing, change
management & ACDOCA is the universal journal.
Important SAP notes are…………. https://support.sap.com/
          • 2148893 - Central Finance: Implementation and Configuration
          • 2184567 - Central Finance: Frequently Asked Questions (FAQ)
          • 2863836 - Allowed Currency Configuration Settings in Central Finance
          • 3194497 - Error Resolution Guides for S/4HANA Central Finance
          • 2610660 - SAP LT Replication Server for Central Finance - Third-Party System Integration 1709, FPS 2 and 1809           
         • 2932517 - Central Finance Third-Party System Interface: Frequently Asked
Questions (FAQ)
                     >>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>END>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>>

entry.title: DigiCert G5 Certificate Warning in /SDF/ALM_DIAGNOSTIC
entry.content:
Earlier this year, my team and I expaded our work with SAP Cloud ALM, integrating it with S4.  During the first configuration, we faced a persistent certificate warning related to DigiCert G5, and the health monitoring integration was impacted.

What made the situation intriguing was that STRUST was correctly maintained, the connection was technically active, yet the warning remained. Since there is already plenty of configuration content available, I decided to document this specific scenario to help others who may encounter the same behavior.

I hope this experience can be useful for people dealing with similar integration scenarios.

Context

During the integration between S/4 and SAP Cloud ALM, we noticed that Health Monitoring data was not updating as expected.

The configuration had been completed properly:

  • Transaction /SDF/ALM_SETUP executed
  • Certificate installed and validated in STRUST

However, when running /SDF/ALM_DIAGNOSTIC, a certificate warning related to DigiCert G5 was still displayed.

Symptom

  • Certificate warning message persists in /SDF/ALM_DIAGNOSTIC
  • Health Monitoring data was not updating consistently
  • Integration technically active but inconsistent

     

Root Cause Analysis

What was actually happening was related to the Health Monitoring framework delivered with certain ST-PI levels. There were inconsistencies in how monitoring data was collected and validated.

SAP Note 3639977 does not directly fix the certificate warning. Instead, it stabilizes several parts of the Health Monitoring logic.

Solution

The issue was resolved after implementing the SAP Note (3639977).

Steps performed:

  1. Implement SAP Note via SNOTE
  2. Transport adjustment 
  3. Re-run /SDF/ALM_DIAGNOSTIC
  4. Deactivate Health Monitoring data collection, wait approximately 20 minutes, and reactivate.
  5. Validate Health Monitoring data in SAP Cloud ALM

After implementation:

  • Monitoring data started updating correctly
  • Health Monitoring became consistent and reliable

Additional Correction (SAP Note 3426408)

After stabilizing the Health Monitoring behavior, the certificate warning /SDF/ALM_DIAGNOSTIC still showing.

To solve that, it was necessary to implement NOTE 3426408. This note enables the required diagnostic checks in the managed system and ensures that the certificate validation in STRUST is properly executed.

Conclusion

After the SAP notes implementatios, the system is now properly integrated and performing the required diagnostic validations as expected.

I hope this small step contributes to the community and that we can continue exchanging knowledge around S/4 and ALM

entry.title: GR Cancellation/reversal issue: "Deficit of PU GR quantity xxxx DAY"
entry.content:
Hi,

When I tried to post a goods cancellation reversal (102 or 122 Movement Type), I got the error 'Deficit of PU IR quantity xxxx DAY'.

My scenario is as follows: 

Our PO has one line; we don't have stock to manage, and we have the total invoiced amount greater than the received amount. And we want to post a GR cancellation to adjust the balance. As we have the flag GR-Bsd Inv already checked, we forced the system using the transactions (OMJJ & OMBZ). We managed to bypass the control, and we reverted two lines. But the system is blocking the reversal of a new line with the new amount.

We tried with different movement type but the error message is the same: 102 & 122. 

My question is why is it not allowing me to post the goods reversal? and how to overcome this issue?

Thanks a lot for your insights and support.

MM (Materials Management)

entry.title: Enterprise AI in Action: SAP Joule Delivers Where Others Don’t
entry.content:
AI is becoming part of everyday enterprise work, from drafting emails to designing complex processes. While ChatGPT and Gemini can generate functional specs and ABAP code, the real question is: “Why use SAP Joule?”
The difference is context – Joule is embedded in S/4HANA, understanding system configuration, business rules, workflows, and live data to deliver outputs that are accurate, compliant, and ready for real-world use. Unlike general-purpose AI, it doesn’t just produce well-written text; it grasps the technical and business landscape to ensure every recommendation aligns with your enterprise environment.
To understand this difference in practical terms, let’s look at two common S/4HANA scenarios: a Purchase Order compliance check and a Sales Order credit validation. On the surface, both appear straightforward – define the requirement, identify the enhancement spot, document the logic, and generate the necessary ABAP code.
A general-purpose AI tool can certainly help structure the functional specification and outline the technical approach. However, the real challenge in enterprise projects is not drafting the document – it is ensuring that the design aligns with the system’s actual configuration, enhancement framework, workflow setup, authorization model, and business data. This is where the distinction between generic AI and embedded, SAP-native intelligence becomes clear.

1. Purchase Order Compliance Check
Business Requirement: Block Purchase Order creation if:
• Vendor is not approved in a custom Z-table
• PO value exceeds 1,000,000
• Purchasing group = “A01”
• Mandatory attachment is missing
Using Chatgpt or Gemini:
• Enhancement via BAdI ME_PROCESS_PO_CUST
• Logic to check vendor approval, PO value, purchasing group, and attachment
• Error messages and test cases
Pros: Clear FS structure, general logic explained
Cons: Generic — it won’t know your actual Z-table structure, workflow configuration, or document type setup. You must feed these details manually.
Using SAP Joule:
• Identifies the correct BAdI or workflow in your S/4 system
• Pulls actual vendor master and custom table fields
• Checks your approval strategy and document type configuration
• Generates FS logic aligned with your live system setup
Pros: System-grounded, accurate, reduces rework, supports compliance

2. Sales Order Credit Validation
Business Requirement: Prevent Sales Order creation if a customer’s credit limit is exceeded.
Using Chatgpt or Gemini:
• Identify a relevant BAdI or user exit
• Check credit exposure against the limit
• Trigger an error message if the limit is exceeded
• Suggest test cases for validation
Pros: Clean structure, well-written, and easy to understand
Cons: Generic — it doesn’t know your actual credit control area, company code setup, or the exact fields and BAdIs in your S/4HANA system. All system-specific details must be manually added.
Using SAP Joule:
• Detect the active BAdI or enhancement spot in your system
• Pull the correct credit control configuration and field names
• Align logic with company code settings and authorization roles
• Suggest tests based on your live system data
Pros: Context-aware, system-aligned, lower risk of errors

Key Differentiators

  1. Verified SAP Knowledge Base: Responses are grounded in SAP's official documentation and verified sources.
  2. SAP-Specific Methodology: Understands SAP implementation approaches, configuration requirements, and technical constraints.
  3. Industry-Specific Compliance: Aligned with SAP's consulting standards and best practices.
  4. SAP Ecosystem Integration: Deep understanding of how SAP modules, technologies, and processes interconnect.

Comparison Table

Capability

SAP Joule for Consultants

ChatGPT

Gemini

Notes

SAP Domain Expertise

Deep, verified SAP knowledge from official sources

General SAP knowledge from public training data

General SAP knowledge with real-time search

Joule is trained on SAP-verified documentation

Template Generation

SAP-specific templates and methodologies

Generic business templates

Generic business templates with Google integration

Templates follow SAP best practices and standards

Functional Specification Writing

SAP implementation-focused with proper technical structure [3],[4]

General functional spec approaches

Structured but generic approaches

Joule understands SAP-specific requirements and constraints

Industry Standards Compliance

SAP best practices and implementation standards

General industry standards

General standards with research capabilities

Aligned with SAP's consulting methodologies

Technical Documentation Structure

SAP-aligned formats (ABAP, configuration paths, transaction codes) [2],[5]

Generic technical documentation

Structured technical docs

Joule provide SAP-specific technical details and paths

Integration Knowledge

Deep SAP ecosystem and module integration expertise

Broad but surface-level SAP integration knowledge

General integration knowledge

Joule understand SAP module interdependencies and best practices

Business Process Understanding

SAP module-specific processes and workflows

General business process knowledge

General business knowledge with research

Joule trained on SAP business process documentation

For functional specification writing in SAP contexts, Joule is the superior choice because:

  • Accuracy: knowledge comes from verified SAP sources rather than general internet training data. 
  • Relevance: understands SAP-specific technical requirements, configuration paths, and implementation constraints. 
  • Structure: can provide proper SAP documentation formats, including transaction codes, customization paths, and technical specifications. 
  • Best Practices: aligned with SAP's consulting methodologies and implementation standards.

We’ve seen how generic AI can draft specs and code, but context matters. In your S/4HANA projects, where has AI helped — and where has it fallen short? Share your experiences, challenges, or tips below!

Source:

SAP Help Portal | SAP Online Help

Joule

Other Blogs:

From Conversational AI to Business AI: Why SAP Jou... - SAP Community

System Conversion to SAP S/4HANA - SAP Community

entry.title: Re: Service Order Determines Wrong Plant for Service Part...
entry.content:
How SAP Determines the Plant for Service Parts

For service parts in a service order, the plant is not inherited directly from the service order header. Instead, SAP determines the plant based on:

Material Master Data
The plant assignment in the product master (material master) is the primary driver. If the material (e.g., Generator Oil) is maintained in plant 1010, the system will default to that plant, even if the service order header is in plant 1020.

Plant Determination Logic
SAP uses the logistics plant determination rules for service parts, which differ from service labor items. The system checks the material master and organizational assignments rather than the employee location or service team.

Employee Service Location / Service Team
These influence the service plant for labor items, but not for service parts. Service parts are always tied to material master plant data.

Why You’re Seeing the Error

Your service order header correctly determines plant 1020. But the service part item (Generator Oil) is maintained in plant 1010 in the material master. This mismatch creates an intercompany posting (1010 → 1020), which is not supported for service parts in Public Cloud. Hence, the release fails with the error message.


How to Ensure Service Parts Use the Same Plant

To align service parts with the service order plant (1020):

1) Extend the Material Master to Plant 1020
Maintain the service part (Generator Oil) in plant 1020. Ensure stock, valuation, and procurement views are set up for 1020.

2) Check Default Plant Determination Settings
In configuration, verify that the default plant determination for service parts points to the same plant as the service order. This is usually controlled by organizational data setup (company code ↔ plant assignments).

3) Cross-Company Assignments
Ensure that the material is not restricted to a different company code’s plant (1010). Service parts must belong to the same company code/plant as the service order.

Key Takeaway

  • Service parts derive their plant from the material master, not from the service order header.
  • To fix your scenario, you need to maintain the service part material in plant 1020 so that the system doesn’t default to 1010 and trigger an intercompany scenario

entry.title: Re: Set visible dependent on selected members in Input Co...
entry.content:
Hello @PaulaSAC ,

What you need is absolutely possible. 

1. Is this possible using an onSelect script on my input control, or do I need to read from a table?

You can use the onSelect method from your input-contol, you don't need to read from a table. 

2. How can I read the selected member from an input control or table?

You can read all about the different functions in the official script reference, but the way to read the values is :

var selectedCountry = InputControl_2.getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers();

Note: Even if your input-selection is set to "Single Selection", the function will always return an array. 

So, to get the value, you have to fetch the first element of that array:

//Get All Values:
var selectedValues = InputControl_2.getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers();
console.log(selectedValues); //Display Array - Only for demonstration :) 

//Get first element in array:
var selectedCountry = selectedValues[0];
console.log(selectedCountry); //Display Selected Value - Only for demonstration :)

Which will give you the value:

You can then use selectedCountry.id in an IF or switch statement.

//Get All Values:
var selectedValues = InputControl_2.getInputControlDataSource().getActiveSelectedMembers();
//Get first element in array:
var selectedCountry = selectedValues[0];
switch (selectedCountry.id) {
	case 'France':
		//do something
		break;
	case 'Germany':
		//do something else
		break;
	default:
		//do something	
}

3. How do I use setVisible to display the correct image?

Now, I don't know how your report is set up, but the "Image" object itself does not have a setVisible method, as you can see here.

However, you can wrap the images in a Panel, which does:

...
case 'France':
		Panel_12.setVisible(true);
		break;
	case 'Germany':
		//do something else
		break;
	default:
		//do something

That will hide the panel and all the content.

 

Hope this helps! 🙂

Indeed the problem is there. However I don't think yet another drop down option is necessary. We already have SAP managed tags that work well enough. They designate SAP product clearly. If SAP managed tags are not used properly, the additional option will also be misused. 

The challenge is to teach users to use the tags properly. People seem not to understand how to use SAP managed tags unfortunately.

Take this question for instance: 

The question is about quality stock management, but it is tagged with "SAP HANA". SAP HANA is a database. There is no quality stock in SAP HANA as such.

Best regards

Dominik Tylczynski

entry.title: Re: S4 Hara BRF+ out not triggered email to buyer.
entry.content:
1. Verify Email Address Consistency
In SU01 and BP (Business Partner), confirm the email address is identical and active. Pay attention to Case sensitivity (some SMTP servers are strict). Extra spaces or hidden characters. Whether the BP email is flagged as “Standard Communication” email.

2. Check APOC Tables
You mentioned the email is visible in APOC_D_ITEM_E. That means the output request was generated. Next, check APOC_D_ITEM_S (status table) to see if the output item was processed successfully or if an error status exists.

3. Review SOST (SAPconnect)
Go to transaction SOST and check if the email was handed over to SAPconnect. If the email is missing here, the issue is before handover (likely determination of recipient). If the email is present but stuck, check the status:

  • Red → error in sending (SMTP issue, invalid address).
  • Yellow → still in queue.
  • Green → successfully sent.

4. Check SCOT Configuration
In SCOT, verify the SMTP node is active and correctly configured. Ensure the domain of the recipient email is allowed and not blocked by routing rules.

5. Compare Working vs. Non‑Working User
Since one user receives emails, compare both users:

  • SU01 → Communication tab: is the email type set to “Business” or “Internet”?
  • BP → Address tab: is the email marked as “Standard”?
  • Authorization: does the non‑working user have authorization to trigger external communication?

6. Check Job Scheduling
Ensure the SAPconnect send job (RSCONN01) is scheduled and running. If the job runs after the email is generated, check whether the non‑working user’s email is picked up.

7. Analyze Application Log
Use transaction SLG1 with object APOC or SFO to check logs for the PO output. Look for warnings/errors related to recipient determination.

8. Test with Alternate Email
Temporarily change the non‑working user’s email to a known working external address. If the email goes through, the issue is with the recipient’s domain or mailbox. If it still fails, the issue is with SAP determination.

Key Takeaway

The fact that the email appears in APOC_D_ITEM_E means BRF+ and OPD are fine.

  • The most likely root cause is either:
    • The email address in BP/SU01 is not flagged correctly as the standard communication email, or
    • The email is failing at SAPconnect (SOST/SCOT) level due to SMTP/domain issues.

entry.title: Any way to determine whether its a CREATE or EDIT operation inside a RAP dynamic Feature Instance
entry.content:
I am having a custom RAP BO Draft enabled Managed application having SalesOrder Header and Composition Sales Item Child.

I have implemented one dynamic feature instance for quantity field (KWMENG) of Item view

field( features : instance ) KWMENG;

Inside the method get_instance_features, I have implemented the logic to make the qty field in the item table as read only which is working perfectly fine

Now what i want is , if the above feature instance is called during CREATE , then I want to skip the logic of making this field read only and if its called using EDIT button , then i want the logic to be applied.

So basically, I need a way to identify inside get_instance_features, whether this method is triggered from CREATE or EDIT button? Can someone kindly help in understanding how to identify this ?

Below is my code for feature instance for disabling the Qty field works fine (but in both CREATE and EDIT operation)

METHOD get_instance_features.
     DATA: lv_str type string.

     lv_str = 'ABC'.

     if requested_features-%field-kwmeng = if_abap_behv=>mk-on.
       "Reading records for selected Key fields
       READ entities of ZR_Sales_Header in local mode
         entity ZS_Header
         fields ( vbeln gbstk )
         with corresponding #( keys )
         result DATA(lt_res_hdr)

         entity ZS_Header by \_SalesOrdItem
         fields ( vbeln posnr kwmeng )
         with corresponding #( keys )
         result DATA(lt_res_itm)
         failed DATA(lt_failed).

       TRY.
         "Fetching the order status from the sales order header (Using 1st record from item table
         "as all items will have same header status
         "DATA(lv_ord_status) = lt_res_hdr[ %tky = lt_res_itm[ 1 ]-%tky-vbeln ]-%data-gbstk.
          DATA(lv_ord_status) = lt_res_hdr[ %tky = lt_res_itm[ 1 ]-%tky ]-%data-gbstk.

        CATCH cx_sy_itab_line_not_found.
         clear lv_ord_status.
       ENDTRY.

       "If order status is not completed
        if lv_ord_status <> 'C'.

           "Fetching alternate line items of order if status is not completed
           result = VALUE #( for ls_res_item in lt_res_itm  
                              ( %tky = ls_res_item-%tky
                                %field-kwmeng = if_abap_behv=>FC-f-read_only            )
                           ).
        endif.
     endif.
  ENDMETHOD.

entry.title: Automatic Workflow Triggers in Signavio
entry.content:
Hello, 

I need a way to trigger a Workflow for a Model automatically, in this case if a process hasn't been modified for a year.

It should work as a rule basically, where the system detects processes that have been last published over a year ago, and starts a workflow that notifies someone for example to check it and confirm that the process is still relevant and up-to-date. I don't think this should be beyond the capabilities of any tool.
The closest I've come to this is the following: creating a report of all processes, filtering them by age in excel, copying the IDs into the form of a Workflow that, using the JavaScript action iterates through all IDs and starts a new case for each model in a different Workflow.

If anyone can provide any Info on Automatic Workflow Triggers or a way to implement this yearly review in Signavio, in any way, I'd really appreciate it, as I struggle to find any other resources online, and from what I've heard it might not even be possible in Signavio. Hence this post.

Thanks in advance and Godspeed.

entry.title: Cross Deployment Unit Issue – Cross-DU Issue: Auto Create ServiceRequest on Prospect Creation
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I have a requirement in SAP Cloud for Customer (C4C) and need guidance on the correct architectural approach.

Business Requirement:

When a Prospect (Customer – standard BO) is created and the Role Code is BUP002, a ServiceRequest (standard BO) should be created automatically. The ServiceRequest must have a specific Processing Type, a predefined Subject (e.g., “Verification Ticket”), and should be linked to the Prospect.

System Landscape:

Customer BO (standard) belongs to the Foundation Deployment Unit.

ServiceRequest BO (standard) belongs to the Service/CRM Deployment Unit.

Problem:

When I try to use ServiceRequest.Create() inside Customer.xbo (AfterModify), I receive the error:

"Access of member 'Create' in a cross-deployment unit is not possible."

Since both are standard BOs in different Deployment Units, direct creation is not allowed.

My Questions:

If both objects are standard BOs, can IC (Integration Component) be used directly between them?

For this scenario, how many Custom BOs are required?

Is one Custom BO in the Service DU enough (as a bridge)?

Or are two Custom BOs required (one in each DU)?

If using a Custom BO in Service DU, how can it be triggered when a Prospect is created in Customer BO, considering cross-DU restrictions still apply?

What is the SAP-recommended best practice for this type of cross-deployment requirement? Should this be implemented using:

Custom BO bridge pattern

Workflow rules

Web Service / OData

Or another approach?

I would like to follow a clean and SAP-compliant design without violating cross-deployment restrictions.

Any guidance on the correct architecture would be appreciated.

Thank you.

C4C Service  C4C Service  SAP CRM, GWI client plug-in SAP Cloud Applications Studio

entry.title: Re: Impact of choosing "standard acct" in document splitt...
entry.content:
Hi @luxman_shankar ,

Yes, using a “constant profit center” as the fallback in SAP’s standard account assignment is a common and fully supported practice, but it comes with some design considerations that are worth thinking through before you implement it across multiple company codes.

If your organization has one controlling area, has centralized finance/CO reporting and wants simple error handling, A single constant profit center is absolutely standard and widely used.

If your organization has multiple company codes with different reporting owners and needs audit traceability per entity and wants cleaner segmentation of derivation failures, then consider company‑code‑specific fallback profit centers via custom derivation.

entry.title: Re: Impact of choosing "standard acct" in document splitt...
entry.content:
Hello @luxman_shankar ,

Using a single fallback profit center for multiple company codes in document splitting is technically supported and commonly used, but it should be carefully monitored to avoid distortions in profit center reporting.

If you choose a constant fallback profit center:

-Ensure it is valid across all company codes.
-Use it only for exception handling.
-Implement monitoring (e.g., periodic report on postings with that profit center).

Many implementations also create a naming convention such as:
PC9999 – Default / Derivation Error Profit Center, so finance teams can easily identify and correct these postings.

 

Question / ScenarioExplanation, Best Practice & SAP Notes
What does “Standard Account Assignment” mean in document splitting?In document splitting configuration, Standard Account Assignment is used as a fallback value when the system cannot derive a splitting characteristic (e.g., Profit Center, Segment). The system assigns the predefined constant value to ensure the document remains balanced.
Using a constant Profit Center when derivation failsYes, this is a common approach. When the system cannot determine the profit center from the source object (cost center, WBS, asset, etc.), the fallback profit center ensures that the document splitting process does not stop with an error.
Scope of the configurationThis setting is client-wide, meaning the same fallback profit center will be applied across all company codes if derivation fails.
Concern: assigning the same fallback profit center for multiple company codesThis is technically allowed and commonly used, especially in global templates. However, you must ensure that the fallback profit center exists and is valid for all company codes where it may be used.
Accounting impactIf the fallback profit center is triggered frequently, financial statements by profit center may become distorted because postings are assigned to the default profit center instead of the correct operational one. Therefore, it should only be used as an exception.
Recommended best practice✔ Use fallback profit center only as a technical safeguard. ✔ Monitor postings hitting the fallback profit center regularly. ✔ Investigate root causes for missing derivations (master data gaps, missing substitutions, incorrect account assignments).
Alternative design approachSome companies create a dedicated “Error / Suspense Profit Center” per company code or per controlling area to better monitor incorrect derivations.
MonitoringBuild reporting to track postings using the fallback profit center so configuration or master data can be corrected.
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help → Document Splitting in New General Ledger SAP Help → Profit Center Derivation and Balancing in Universal Journal
Relevant SAP Notes (Verify Applicability)📌 SAP Note 891144 – Document Splitting: Standard Account Assignment Logic 📌 SAP Note 1155429 – Profit Center Derivation in Document Splitting 📌 SAP Note 1939127 – Balancing Fields in Document Splitting 📌 SAP Note 2344012 – Document Splitting Behavior in S/4HANA

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Re: WHT (Withholding Tax) input when create PR (Purchase...
entry.content:
Hello @ailliyuni ,

For automatic WHT calculation during MIRO:

-Use Extended Withholding Tax configuration as primary mechanism
-Implement BAdI MRM_WT_SPLIT_UPDATE if additional derivation logic is required
-Avoid using BAPI or GGB0 for WHT calculation logic

Flow:

PO → MIRO → BAdI reads business rules → populate WHT type/code → SAP calculates WHT.

Use BAdI: MRM_WT_SPLIT_UPDATE

 

Requirement / QuestionExplanation, Technical Options & SAP Notes
Requirement: automatic WHT calculation when parking/posting invoice (MIR7 / MIRO)In standard SAP, Extended Withholding Tax (EWT) calculates WHT automatically during MIRO posting based on vendor master configuration and withholding tax setup.
Does SAP support line-item WHT calculation in MIRO?Yes. When Extended Withholding Tax is active, SAP calculates WHT at document level or line-item level depending on configuration of withholding tax type and base amount definition.
Where WHT logic is normally determinedWHT calculation uses: • Vendor master data (LFBW) • Company code WHT configuration • Withholding tax type and code • Base amount (invoice line items)
If additional logic is requiredIf business logic requires deriving WHT based on PO attributes, material group, cost center, etc., enhancement is required during MIRO processing.
Recommended BAdI for WHT manipulationBAdI MRM_WT_SPLIT_UPDATE allows modification or creation of withholding tax lines during MIRO processing. This BAdI is specifically designed for WHT adjustments in Logistics Invoice Verification.
Other possible enhancement spots• BAdI MRM_HEADER_DEFAULT – default header values • BAdI MRM_ITEM_DEFAULT – manipulate invoice item values before posting
Can BAPI be used for this?❌ BAPI is not recommended for controlling WHT logic inside MIRO because invoice verification processing uses internal structures. Enhancements must be implemented using BAdI or exits.
Can Validation (GGB0) determine WHT?❌ GGB0 validation can only check conditions and raise messages. It cannot determine or calculate withholding tax values dynamically.
Typical enhancement logic1️⃣ During MIRO item processing read PO reference 2️⃣ Determine WHT type/code based on business rule 3️⃣ Update WHT structure via MRM_WT_SPLIT_UPDATE 4️⃣ Allow SAP to perform standard WHT calculation
Data tables involvedVendor WHT setup stored in LFBW. Calculated WHT amounts stored in accounting document tables (BSEG / ACDOCA equivalent structures).
SAP Help ReferenceSAP Help → Extended Withholding Tax in Accounts Payable SAP Help → Logistics Invoice Verification Enhancements
Relevant SAP Notes (Verify Applicability)📌 SAP Note 830717 – Extended Withholding Tax Determination 📌 SAP Note 1156325 – Withholding Tax Processing in Invoice Verification 📌 SAP Note 1548013 – WHT Handling in MM Invoice Verification 📌 SAP Note 2220152 – Extended Withholding Tax in S/4HANA

If you find my response helpful, share your feedback. This motivates me to serve you better.
Appreciate your support.
Thanks,
Divyesh

entry.title: Timesheet Time Entry with Multiple Activity Allocation & Activity Derivation Functionality
entry.content:
Hello dear SAP Gurus,

   In our customer we need an overhead allocation functionality with activity types to post project related indirect costs from cost center to WBS elements. The design expectation is using activity types sourced by timesheet, since costing sheet is not flexible and determination of overhead rates can not be done because of less correlation with any base account group, it is off the table.  Let me make our scenario clearer :

-> Project planning held and source demand took place,

-> A worker post timesheet entry and activity T001 (Labor) has allocated, journal entry created via 943* -> 100 EUR credits CC, debits WBS

Because our client would want to observe general project costs and deprecitation ( which are not going to run onto WBS' but CC's) during the period ( at least close to the actual expectation as possible via cost rates) and costing sheet isn't functional for them, they would also like to observe at least two other activities from a single timesheet entry; lets say T002 (Depr.) and T003 (GPC) 

The same can be managed on work center level on acitivity confirmation scnearios in production, yet as far as we understand that timesheet entries have 1:1 relations with activity types which are determined by the demand and the worker service cost level. Also, template allocation functions are not available in Cloud Public edition, we can't manage to derivate multiple activity allocations from a template. 

My question is, is there such functionality of multiple activity allocation from single timesheet entry supported in standard ? If not, is there a tool to derivate multiple activity allocations with reference to source entry ? 

And in general, is there another procedure under this scope to allocate overheads from cost centers to WBS elements ? Briefly, expectation is tracking activity allocations in projects similar to production.

   Thanks in advance for your guidance and support, please ask if you need more details on the requested info.

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition SAP Timesheet SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition Finance

Hi @JessieCheah and SAP Product Team,

Thanks for the excellent SAP Fiori Elements Feature Showcase with RAP  and for continuously updating it with new releases. The annotation-driven V4 app that is transactional and draft-enabled has been a great resource to explore the capabilities of Fiori Elements with RAP.

I see updates mentioned for 2302, 2308, and 2402 releases, which were very helpful. However, it appears that there haven’t been updates to the showcase app since the 2023 wave. entry.title: EAM_TASKLIST_CREATE – “Service package required” error when creating multiple task lists sequentiall
entry.content:
I am trying to create multiple maintenance task lists programmatically using EAM_TASKLIST_CREATE in ABAP. 

Scenario:

  • Two maint. plans are in a loop and for each i am executing EAM_TASKLIST_CREATE, POST and COMMIT.  
  • Creating two maintenance task lists sequentially in the same program run. 
  • Both contain service operations. 
  • Each plan works correctly when executed separately.

Problem:

  • When executed one after another in the same program, the second task list fails during POST with error:

" Operation ... requires a service package! ".  But this operation does not need a service package.

  • Debugging shows that in the method GET_SERVICE_PACKAGE , the variables are:

my_ext_header-packno = 0000397416    
lv_packno = 0000397313

This causes an early RETURN and service data is not filled.  my_ext_header-packno should be initial or the same like lv_packno for the service data to be filled. packno = 0000397416 is from the first plan.

What I have tried:

  • Clearing all internal tables (gt_opr_ins, gt_spack_lines, etc.) before second creation.

  • Executing BAPI_TRANSACTION_COMMIT after the first creation.

  • Using DESTINATION 'NONE' in the function call.

  • Wrapping the call in a separate RFC-enabled wrapper.

  • Verified that gt_opr_ins-packno = 0000000000 for both plans.

  • Both plans work individually without errors.

Questions:

  • What is the recommended way to programmatically create multiple task lists with service operations in one run?

entry.title: Re: GR Cancellation/reversal issue: "Deficit of PU GR qua...
entry.content:
Why the error occurs
Your PO line has invoices posted greater than the received quantity. When you try to cancel a GR (movement type 102 or 122), the system would reduce the received quantity further. That would make the situation even worse: invoiced > received by an even larger margin. SAP blocks this with the “Deficit of PU IR quantity” error to ensure consistency between GR and invoice verification (GR‑based IV).

How to Resolve
You cannot bypass this check with OMJJ/OMBZ alone — those settings only control tolerance and message behavior. The root cause is the invoice quantity > GR quantity imbalance. To fix:

1) Reverse the Invoice First
Use MR8M (Cancel Invoice Document) or MRIS (Settle Invoices) depending on your process. This reduces the invoiced quantity so that GR cancellation becomes possible.

2) Then Reverse the GR.
Once the invoice is canceled, you can post the GR reversal (102 or 122). The system will allow it because invoiced ≤ received.

3) Alternative: Adjust via Credit Memo
If you cannot cancel the invoice (e.g., already paid), post a credit memo against the PO to bring the invoiced quantity back in line. After that, the GR reversal can be processed.

entry.title: Seeking Configuration Points for Colombia DRC (F350, Income Tax, and VAT) - S/4HANA Cloud Public Edi
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I am currently working on a localized implementation for Colombia using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602.

While navigating the Central Business Configuration (CBC) and Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC), I have successfully identified the configuration points and SSCUIs required to link the "Información Exógena" formats (DIAN 1001 to 1009).

However, I am having trouble locating the specific configuration nodes or SSCUIs for the following statutory reports:

  1. Formato 350 (Monthly Withholding Tax Declaration).

  2. Income Tax Declaration (Declaración de Renta).

  3. VAT Declaration (IVA - Formulario 300).

I have already explored the Tax Box Framework (SSCUIs 103011, 103012, and 103013), but it is not clear if these specific forms follow the same mapping logic as the Exógena formats or if they require a different setup within the "Setting Up Your Statutory Reporting" (102220) activity for this specific release.

Could anyone point me toward the correct configuration path or provide the specific SSCUI IDs for these forms in the 2602 version?

Thanks in advance for your help!

Best regards,

Juan Sebastian Valencia SAP Consultant | Structum Ingeniería S.A.

entry.title: How to access Withholding Tax Summaries and Cumulative Reports in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community experts,

We are currently working on SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition 2602 with the Colombia Localization active. We need to identify the standard Fiori applications or reports to visualize a detailed breakdown and cumulative summary of withholding taxes (Income Tax, VAT, and ICA) by tax indicator for a specific period.

Specifically, we are looking for a view that provides:

  1. Cumulative values grouped by Withholding Tax Type and Indicator.

  2. Transactional details including Journal Entries (Asientos contables), Company Code (Sociedad), and Base Amounts.

  3. Traceability from the summary down to the specific accounting document.

We have explored the Manage Withholding Tax Items (F5428) app, but it seems to be missing from our Launchpad even when searching by ID.

Our questions are:

  • What is the exact Business Role and Business Catalog required in release 2602 to access withholding tax summaries for Colombia?

  • Is the app F5428 the standard tool for this version, or has it been replaced/integrated into the Document and Reporting Compliance (DRC) framework (e.g., via Run Statutory Reports - F1515)?

  • Are there any specific SSCUI configurations (like activating the Withholding Tax Reporting Date - 105376) that are mandatory to make these records visible in the reporting apps?

Any guidance on the "Best Practice" path to get a reliable tax list for reconciliation before legal submission would be greatly appreciated.

Best regards, Juan Sebastian Valencia
